name: Xaintrailles to Pompiey Landscape Trail
description: Hike a moderate 6.1-mile (9.8 km) loop around the historic Château de Xaintrailles, gaining 472 feet (144 metres) in 2h 40m.
region_intro: Hiking around Xaintrailles offers diverse landscapes and panoramic views from its position as the highest point in Albret. The region features a mix of one-third forest and two-thirds farmland, including pine forests and vineyards. From Xaintrailles, situated on a 190-meter hill, views extend over the Garonne plain, the Baïse plain, and the Brulhois hillsides. Best hiking trails around Xaintrailles The most popular hiking route is Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses varied terrain, including forests and open farmland. Another top favourite among local hikers is Xaintrailles to Pompiey Landscape Trail, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding plains and hills, with an ascent of 144 meters. Local hikers also love the Château d'Ambrus – Ambrus loop, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Xaintrailles is defined by elevated viewpoints, pine forests, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Xaintrailles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Xaintrailles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Xaintrailles?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes available in the Xaintrailles area. These trails offer a variety of experiences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging excursions, allowing you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Lot-et-Garonne region.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Xaintrailles?
A: Xaintrailles, being the highest point in Albret, offers exceptional panoramic views. You'll encounter a mix of one-third forest and two-thirds farmland, including verdant plains, vineyards, and shaded pine forests. From elevated points, you can see the Garonne plain, the Baïse plain, and the Brulhois hillsides.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Xaintrailles?
A: Yes, Xaintrailles offers several easy hiking routes. For instance, the region has 21 trails classified as easy. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24832450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide of Vianne – Vianne Tower - loop from Montgaillard-en-Albret&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.1 km long and features a moderate elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Xaintrailles incorporate historical and natural landmarks. The prominent &lt;a href="https://albret-tourisme.com/en/xaintrailles/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Xaintrailles&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century castle, is often a focal point. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henry IV's Mill&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4718016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nérac Old Town and Château d'Henri IV&lt;/a&gt; on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20638648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbaste Roman Bridge – Henry IV's Mill loop from Lavardac&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Xaintrailles area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Xaintrailles are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996710867/chateau-d-ambrus-ambrus-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château d'Ambrus – Ambrus loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.1 km trail that takes you through rural landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Xaintrailles?
A: The hiking trails in Xaintrailles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the peaceful escape from urban areas, and the exceptional panoramic views from the region's elevated points.

Q: Is it possible to reach the hiking trails in Xaintrailles using public transport?
A: While Xaintrailles is a rural area, public transport options may be limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to access the various trailheads. For specific routes, checking local bus schedules or ride-sharing services in nearby larger towns like Nérac or Lavardac might be an option, but direct access to all trailheads via public transport is not guaranteed.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Xaintrailles?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails in Xaintrailles, especially those that pass through natural landscapes and forests. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly when passing through farmland or near livestock, and to respect local regulations and private property signs.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on trails around Xaintrailles?
A: Given Xaintrailles's position on a 190-meter hill, many trails involve some elevation gain, contributing to the panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000303229/xaintrailles-to-pompiey-landscape-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xaintrailles to Pompiey Landscape Trail&lt;/a&gt; has an ascent of approximately 144 meters, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000272479/barbaste-beas-and-lausseignan-hike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike&lt;/a&gt; features an elevation gain of around 258 meters.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer the best panoramic views?
A: Xaintrailles itself is known for its exceptional panoramic views as the highest point in Albret. Many trails starting from or passing through the village will offer vistas of the Garonne plain, Baïse plain, Brulhois hillsides, and the Landes forest. Look for routes that ascend to higher ground or pass through open areas for the best viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Xaintrailles?
A: The Lot-et-Garonne region generally enjoys pleasant weather for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, with blooming wildflowers or vibrant fall foliage. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, especially on shaded forest paths. Winter hiking is also possible, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Xaintrailles well-marked?
A: Yes, the hiking trails in the Xaintrailles region are generally well-marked, providing a peaceful and straightforward experience for walkers. This makes it easier to navigate and enjoy the natural beauty without concerns about getting lost.
tips: The oldest part of the castle is the 13th century keep, to which windows were added in the 15th century. It was the residence at the time of Jean Poton de Xaintrailles who was a companion of Joan of Arc.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The starting point for the loop trail is conveniently located next to a parking lot, ensuring easy access for hikers. You can begin your hike directly from there.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered to be of moderate difficulty. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, but the paths are generally accessible.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain offers picturesque and changing landscapes characteristic of the Lot-et-Garonne region. While paths are generally accessible, some uneven sections may require hikers to be careful where they place their feet.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The most significant highlight is the historic Xaintrailles Castle, a feudal site dating back to the 12th century. The current castle was constructed in the 15th century and can be visited freely, offering a glimpse into its rich past.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits, and there are no entrance fees to hike this trail. The castle site itself can also be visited freely.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other official trails. Notably, it covers about 92% of the "De Xaintrailles à Pompiey, lectures de paysage" trail and about 25% of "Xaintrailles, la balade du château".

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with young children or strollers?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and some uneven sections, the trail may not be ideal for very young children or strollers. Shorter, easier itineraries like "Xaintrailles, la balade du château" (4.6 km) might be more suitable for families.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The village of Xaintrailles, where the trail is located, may offer local amenities. For more options, you might consider nearby towns in the Lot-et-Garonne region.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, as some sections can be uneven. Bring sufficient water, sun protection, and a light jacket, as conditions can change. A map or GPS device is also recommended.
